summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on June 10, 2025. Key actions included accepting two firefighter/paramedic resignations, approving a resolution to authorize the purchase of a new fire truck (quint), hiring two part-time firefighter EMTs, declaring surplus items for sale on GovDeals, and scheduling a special zoning workshop for June 25, 2025. Trustees also recognized fiscal officer Laura for her outstanding work during the biannual audit and expressed condolences to the Swift Pappas family.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for May 27 through June 10, 2025
Declared surplus/obsolete items for sale on GovDeals (projector, armrests, old chair, etc.)
Scheduled special meeting/zoning workshop for June 25, 2025 at 6 PM with Zoning Commission, BZA, and Attorney Kevin Maloff
Accepted resignation of full-time firefighter paramedic Ryan Petronio effective June 21, 2025
Accepted resignation of full-time firefighter paramedic Brandon Kilgore effective June 16, 2025
Approved Resolution R-013-2025 authorizing purchase of a new fire truck (quint)
Adjourned into executive session at 6:15 PM per ORC 121.22G1
Hired part-time firefighter EMT Josh Libsdorf at $17.50/hr effective June 15, 2025
Hired part-time firefighter EMT Ryan West at $17.50/hr effective June 15, 2025
action_items: Administrator Rose and Chief Myers to sign documents related to the quint fire truck purchase
Evan to continue cleaning out audio/video room and list surplus items on GovDeals
Administrator Rose to coordinate zoning workshop on June 25 with Zoning Commission, BZA, Attorney Kevin Maloff, and Director Langenheim
key_amounts: $17.50/hr pay rate for part-time firefighter EMT Josh Libsdorf
$17.50/hr pay rate for part-time firefighter EMT Ryan West
$100 donation from Ken Lane to the fire department
notable_topics: CPA class number 13 graduation with 4 graduates
Authorization to purchase new fire truck (quint)
Scheduling zoning workshop with Zoning Commission, BZA, and attorney
Upcoming community events: Millionare (June 27 - July 7), CPAAA fundraiser at Sprinkles (July 2), Faith Family Fun fireworks at Goshen High School
attendees: Kevin Maloff (Attorney, invited to zoning workshop)
notes: Executive session held from approximately 6:15 PM to 6:45 PM regarding appointment/employment/compensation of a public employee. Ken Lane, a former fireman, insisted on donating $100 to the fire department during cleanup day. Brandon Kilgore's resignation was attributed to unaffordable local housing and a 3.5-hour commute from Cleveland. Ryan Petronio is relocating to Idaho due to his wife's job opportunity.
title: BOT 6-10-25
